editor.title: Editor
editor.subtitle: Build your own cases
editor.name: Case name
editor.windowHint: Click near the edge of a tile to place a window there. Click again to remove it.
editor.doorHint: Click near the edge of a tile to place a door there (counts from both sides). Click again to remove it.
editor.suspectsSoon: Suspects & clues come in the next stage.
editor.saveHint: 'Keep' stores it in the browser (shows up under 'Open case'). 'As JSON' downloads a file — drop it into the levels folder.
editor.resultOkNoMurderer: Solvable & unique, but no single murderer.

err.notCandidate: No, not here — that's not a possible tile for {{name}}.
err.holdToPlace: Press and hold for ~1 second to place (not just a tap).
err.tapForNote: Here just tap briefly to leave a note.

welcome.title: Welcome, detective!
welcome.body: Last night there was a murder. Let's solve this case together — I'll guide you step by step. Tap »Next«.

goal.title: Your goal
goal.body: Find the murderer: work out who was in the same room as the victim. Whoever is alone with the victim is the murderer.

rules.title: The rules
rules.body:
• Each person is in a different row AND column.
• Only beds, chairs and carpets can be occupied.
• Tables, TVs, plants, shelves and boxes are blocked.
• The victim is on the last free tile.

board.title: The crime scene
board.body: This is the crime scene — a 4×4 grid with two rooms: Living Room (top) and Bedroom (bottom). Hover over an object to see its name.

suspects.title: The suspects
suspects.body: Here are the suspects with their statements: Alex was beside a table, Bernd on a bed, Carmen by a window. (On mobile they're below the board.)

terms.title: Bold = important
terms.body: Objects and key words are bold. Hover over a word like »beside« or »alone« to see exactly what it means.

selectA.title: Select Alex
selectA.body: Let's start with Alex. Tap his card to select him.

noteA.title: Where can Alex be?
noteA.body: Alex was beside a table — the blue-outlined tiles are possible. Tap them all briefly to note a small A there.

selectB.title: Select Bernd
selectB.body: On to Bernd. Tap his card.

noteB.title: Where can Bernd be?
noteB.body: Bernd was on a bed — that's walkable. Briefly tap the blue-outlined beds to note a small B.

placeC.title: Place Carmen
placeC.body: Carmen must be by a window — only ONE tile is left! Press and hold that tile for ~1 second to place her there.

locked.title: Row & column locked
locked.body: Look closely: Carmen's row and column are now crossed out — nobody else can stand there. That rules out one of the two beds for Bernd.

selectB2.title: Select Bernd again
selectB2.body: Now Bernd's bed is clear. Select Bernd again.

placeB.title: Place Bernd
placeB.body: Only one bed is possible now. Press and hold it for ~1 second to place Bernd.

selectA2.title: Select Alex
selectA2.body: And now Alex — only one tile is left for him too. Select Alex.

placeA.title: Place Alex
placeA.body: Place Alex on the only remaining tile beside the table (press and hold for ~1 second).

tools.title: Your tools
tools.body: Quick tour of the tools: X tool (block tiles), Reset (hold), Undo, Hint and Solve. (On mobile they're a bar at the bottom.)

selectV.title: Select the victim
selectV.body: Only the victim is left. Tap Olli (the victim).

placeV.title: Place the victim
placeV.body: The victim is on the last free tile. Press and hold it for ~1 second to place Olli.

solve.title: Solve!
solve.body: Done — everyone is placed! Now press »Solve«. Whoever is alone with the victim is the murderer.

generate.title: Generate a level
generate.subtitle: Compose your case
generate.hint: Larger boards and "Hard" can take a few seconds.
generate.failed: No level found — please try again or pick different settings.

game.selectPrompt: Tap to select · long-press to place

result.loseBody: At least one person is misplaced. Keep at it!
result.nameLabel: What should the level be called?

clue.uniqueNearWindow: {{subject}} was {{neg}}[[the only person beside a window:uniqueNearWindow]]
clue.insideXor: {{subject}} and {{target}} were neither both [[inside:inside]] nor both [[outside:outside]]
clue.aloneWithPeople: {{subject}} was alone with {{target}} and a woman
clue.aloneWithDir: {{subject}} was alone with {{target}} and a woman {{direction}} of them

rule.aloneWithVictim: The victim must be alone in a room with exactly one suspect.

tip.onObject: Standing directly on this object (it is walkable).
tip.nearObject: Right next to it — same room, one tile left, right, above or below the object.
tip.nearWindow: On the tile right at a window (the window sits on that tile's wall).
tip.corner: Corner tile: at least two walls touch / enclose the tile.
tip.atWall: At least one side of the tile borders a wall (board edge or another room).
tip.alone: Truly alone in the room — no other suspect, and not the victim either.
tip.aloneWith: Only this one person is otherwise in the room (the victim doesn't count).
tip.uniqueOnObject: The only person standing on such an object.
tip.uniqueNearWindow: The only person standing beside a window.
tip.sameRoom: In the same room as this person.
tip.direction: Lies in this compass direction from the named person (north = further up, etc.).
tip.offset: Exactly that many tiles in this direction from the target person.
tip.roomAttrNone: Nobody else in the same room has this trait.
tip.roomAttrSome: At least one person in the same room has this trait.
tip.roomAttrAll: Everyone in the same room has this trait.
tip.inRow: The named row (counted from the top).
tip.inCol: The named column (counted from the left).
tip.nearDoor: On the tile right at a door (doors count from both sides).
tip.inside: In an indoor room (not pasture, yard or garden).
tip.outside: In an outdoor area (pasture, yard or garden).
tip.notAlone: At least one other person is in the same room.

step.nakedSingle: {{name}} must be on {{cell}} – the only possible cell.
step.uniqueConstraint: Only {{name}} may stand there – others excluded from those cells.
step.hiddenSingleRow: Only {{name}} can be in row {{line}} – so placed there.
step.hiddenSingleCol: Only {{name}} can be in column {{line}} – so placed there.
step.nakedGroupRows: {{people}}: only rows {{lines}} possible – blocked for others.
step.nakedGroupCols: {{people}}: only columns {{lines}} possible – blocked for others.
step.rectangle: Wherever {{name}} ends up, {{cell}} stays blocked – nobody can be there.
step.insideXor: {{other}} is {{otherSide}}, so {{name}} is not {{otherSide}} – cells excluded.
step.boardCountFull: Already {{count}} person on {{object}} – excluded for everyone else.
step.boardCountConfine: Only {{name}} can be on {{object}} – so must go there.
step.relationalSameRoom: {{name}} is in the same room as {{target}} – other rooms removed.
step.aloneExcludeRoom: {{name}} must be alone, but someone is already in the {{room}} – so not there.
step.aloneReserve: {{name}} is alone in the {{room}} – everyone else is excluded from it.
step.attrExcludeRoom: No one in {{name}}'s room may have {{attribute}}; the {{room}} has such a person – so not there.
step.attrReserve: {{name}} is in the {{room}}, which must have no {{attribute}} – such people are excluded.
step.murderVictimNotRoom: The {{room}} already holds {{count}} suspects – the victim wasn't there (they were alone with the murderer).
step.murderIdentified: The victim is in the {{room}}; {{name}} is there too – so {{name}} is the murderer, everyone else leaves the {{room}}.
step.murderConfine: The victim is in the {{room}}; only {{name}} can be there – {{name}} must go there (alone with the victim).
step.forcing: Assuming {{name}} on {{cell}} leads to a contradiction – cell excluded.
step.satPlace: {{name}} must be on {{cell}} – every other cell leads to a contradiction.
step.satExclude: {{count}} cells lead to a contradiction for {{name}} – excluded.
step.victim: The victim {{name}} remains on the last free cell {{cell}}.
step.murderer: Murderer: {{name}} was alone with the victim in the {{room}}.
step.murdererAmbiguous: No unique murderer: {{count}} suspects in the {{room}}.
step.stuck: Stuck: {{count}} person(s) unresolved (technique not yet implemented).

contra.empty: {{name}} would then have no possible cell left – contradiction.
contra.murder: the victim could then no longer be alone with anyone – contradiction.
contra.general: the arrangement then no longer works out – contradiction.
contra.exhausted: the rest then can't be completed (all possibilities checked) – contradiction.
